XCalculateSignatureUpdate

The XCalculateSignatureUpdate function adds data to a save game or other data set for which a signature is being calculated.

DWORD XCalculateSignatureUpdate(
  HANDLE hCalcSig,
  const BYTE *pbData,
  ULONG cbData
);

Parameters

hCalcSig
[in] Handle to the signature being calculated. The handle must have been returned by a previous call to XCalculateSignatureBegin.
pbData
[in] Pointer to the buffer containing the data for which the signature is to be calculated. This may be the entire save game (for example), or may be only a portion of the data, the remainder being added with subsequent calls to XCalculateSignatureUpdate.
cbData
[in] Number of bytes of data in pbData to add.

Return Values

If the function succeeds, the return value is ERROR_SUCCESS.

If the function fails, the return value is an error code defined in winerror.h. The function does not use SetLastError to set the calling thread's last-error code.

Remarks

XCalculateSignatureUpdate adds data to a data set for which a signature is being calculated. A single call can be used to add all of the data of interest, or multiple calls can be made to add portions of data, each of which are to be included in the signature.

XCalculateSignatureBegin prepares a signature context and returns a handle to that context. XCalculateSignatureUpdate adds data to the context to be included in the hash. When all data has been added, call XCalculateSignatureEnd to retrieve the signed hash value (the signature) and to free the resources associated with the signature context.

The data must be added in the same order each time the signature is calculated, otherwise the signatures will not match. For example, if XCalculateSignatureUpdate was first called to add the data for the player's character, then called again to add the data for the current level, that same order must be followed when later loading the save game back in and calculating the save game's signature to verify its authenticity.

XCalculateSignatureUpdate can process approximately 35 MB of data per second.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xbox.h, for include use Xtl.h.
Import Library: Use Xapilib.lib

See Also

Storage Overview, Storage Functions | XCalculateSignatureBegin, XCalculateSignatureEnd
